text: Joseph could no longer control himself before his attendants and cried out, have everyone leave my presence; so no one else was there when he revealed himself.
He wept so loudly the Egyptians and Pharaoh's household heard about it.
Joseph said to his brothers, I am Joseph; is my father still living? They could not answer, they were so terrified.
Come close to me, he said; when they did, he said, I am your brother Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt.
Do not be distressed or angry with yourselves for selling me here, because it was to save lives that God sent me ahead of you.
For two years now there has been famine, and there will be five more years without plowing or harvesting.
God sent me ahead of you to preserve for you a remnant on earth and to save your lives by a great deliverance.
So it was not you who sent me here, but God; he made me father to Pharaoh, lord of his household, and ruler of all Egypt.
Hurry back to my father and say, your son Joseph says God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me without delay.
You will live in Goshen, near me, you and your children, grandchildren, flocks, herds, and everything you have.
I will provide for you there during the remaining five years of famine, so you and your household won't become destitute.
You can see for yourselves, and Benjamin too, that it is really I who speak to you.
Tell my father about all my honor in Egypt and everything you've seen; hurry and bring him down here.
He embraced his brother Benjamin and wept, and Benjamin embraced him weeping.
He kissed all his brothers and wept over them; after that his brothers talked with him.
When word reached Pharaoh's palace that Joseph's brothers had come, Pharaoh and his officials were pleased.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, tell your brothers to load their animals and return to Canaan.
Bring your father and your families back to me; I will give you the best of Egypt's land and you can enjoy the fat of the land.
You are also directed to tell them, take some carts from Egypt for your children and wives, and bring your father back.
Don't worry about your belongings, since the best of all Egypt will be yours.
So the sons of Israel did this; Joseph gave them carts, as Pharaoh had commanded, and supplies for the journey.
To each of them he gave new clothing, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five sets of clothes.
To his father he sent ten donkeys loaded with the best of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and provisions for his journey.
He sent his brothers away, and as they left he told them not to quarrel on the way.
They went up from Egypt and came to their father Jacob in Canaan.
They told him, Joseph is still alive; in fact he is ruler of all Egypt; Jacob was stunned and did not believe them.
But when they told him everything Joseph had said, and he saw the carts Joseph sent, Jacob's spirit revived.
Israel said, I'm convinced; my son Joseph is still alive; I will go see him before I die.
